compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can complete a sprint epic and get a minor version bump via --bump minor
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Update sprint skill Step 13 to pass --bump minor to merge-to-main.sh instead of calling bump-version.sh --minor directly. Remove the sprint-skip note referencing COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b.
**Why**: Centralizes version bumping in merge-to-main.sh so sprint epic completions also avoid version conflicts and use the merge-time bump path.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint SKILL.md Step 13 update, removing direct bump-version.sh --minor call, removing sprint-skip note referencing Step 3b
- OUT: merge-to-main.sh changes (story a0a3-29f8), documentation (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint skill Step 13 passes --bump minor to merge-to-main.sh instead of calling bump-version.sh --minor directly
  <- Satisfies: Sprint skills epic-completion minor bump (Step 13) is replaced with passing --bump minor to merge-to-main.sh
- When this story is complete, the sprint-skip note referencing COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b is removed from Step 13 (dangling reference since S1 removes Step 3b)
  <- Satisfies: COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b (patch version bump) is removed entirely (cross-reference cleanup)
- When this story is complete, worktree-branch epic completion commits no longer contain version changes — the version bump is deferred to merge time
  <- Satisfies: Every merge to main produces exactly one version increment
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Verify that sprint-orchestrated merges produce minor bumps, not patch bumps
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Sprint Phase 9 now passes --bump minor to merge-to-main.sh
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
